examples.title: Algorithm: FIFO Page Replacement
examples.description: Implements First-In-First-Out page replacement using queue structure
examples.code:
ALGORITHM FIFO
BEGIN
 Initialize queue for frames
 Initialize page_fault = 0
 
 FOR each page IN reference_string:
 IF page NOT IN queue:
 page_fault += 1
 IF queue.size() < num_frames:
 queue.enqueue(page)
 ELSE:
 queue.dequeue()
 queue.enqueue(page)
 END IF
 END IF
 END FOR
 RETURN page_fault
END
examples.explanation:
1. Queue maintains insertion order
2. Oldest page is at front of queue
3. Page faults counted when new pages loaded

examples.title: Algorithm: Optimal Page Replacement
examples.description: Theoretical optimal algorithm using future knowledge of references
examples.code:
ALGORITHM OPTIMAL
BEGIN
 Initialize frame_set
 Initialize page_fault = 0
 
 FOR i FROM 0 TO length(reference_string)-1:
 page = reference_string[i]
 IF page NOT IN frame_set:
 page_fault += 1
 IF frame_set.size() < num_frames:
 frame_set.add(page)
 ELSE:
 farthest = -1
 victim = -1
 FOR p IN frame_set:
 next_use = INFINITY
 FOR j FROM i+1 TO length(reference_string)-1:
 IF reference_string[j] == p:
 next_use = j
 BREAK
 END IF
 END FOR
 IF next_use > farthest:
 farthest = next_use
 victim = p
 END IF
 END FOR
 frame_set.remove(victim)
 frame_set.add(page)
 END IF
 END IF
 END FOR
 RETURN page_fault
END
examples.explanation:
1. For each page fault, find page with farthest next use
2. Requires future reference string knowledge
3. Not implementable in practice but serves as benchmark

examples.title: Algorithm: LRU Page Replacement
examples.description: Tracks least recently used pages using ordered list
examples.code:
ALGORITHM LRU
BEGIN
 Initialize list for frames
 Initialize page_fault = 0
 
 FOR each page IN reference_string:
 IF page IN list:
 list.remove(page)
 list.insert(0, page)
 ELSE:
 page_fault += 1
 IF list.size() < num_frames:
 list.insert(0, page)
 ELSE:
 list.remove_last()
 list.insert(0, page)
 END IF
 END IF
 END FOR
 RETURN page_fault
END
examples.explanation:
1. Recently used pages moved to list head
2. Last element is least recently used
3. Maintains usage order through list manipulation

examples.title: Algorithm: Clock (Second Chance)
examples.description: Approximates LRU using circular buffer and reference bits
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CLOCK
BEGIN
 Initialize circular buffer of frames
 Initialize pointer = 0
 Initialize page_fault = 0
 
 FOR each page IN reference_string:
 IF page NOT IN buffer:
 page_fault += 1
 WHILE True:
 current_frame = buffer[pointer]
 IF current_frame.reference_bit == 0:
 replace current_frame with new page
 pointer = (pointer + 1) % num_frames
 BREAK
 ELSE:
 current_frame.reference_bit = 0
 pointer = (pointer + 1) % num_frames
 END IF
 END WHILE
 ELSE:
 buffer.find(page).reference_bit = 1
 END IF
 END FOR
 RETURN page_fault
END
examples.explanation:
1. Circular buffer with reference bits
2. Pages with bit=0 are replaced immediately
3. Pages with bit=1 get second chance (bit reset to 0)
4. Pointer cycles through frames continuously
